title: Market Madness: Palantir, Walmart, & other hot stock picks
summary: In the second installment of Yahoo Finance's "Market Madness" bracket tournament, Zack's Investment Management chief market strategist Brian Mulberry joins Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland to make the case for this round of corporate match-ups, which includes Robinhood (HOOD) vs. UnitedHealth (UNH) Palantir (PLTR) vs. Procter & Gamble (PG), General Motors (GM) vs. Eli Lilly (LLY), Walmart (WMT) vs. Uber (UBER), and more.

title: Truckers love Tesla's electric semi-truck. Here's why.
summary: Tesla (TSLA) is launching its electric semi-truck. The vehicle is currently undergoing trucker pilot test. Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramanian chats with Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland about all the details.

title: Dow Jones Jumps Late After Sell-Off As Trump Mulls 'Winding Down' Iran War
summary: The major indexes broke long-term support as oil prices and bond yields soar. President Trump said late Friday he's mulling "winding down" the Iran war hours after saying that was not "acceptable."

title: Musk faces $2.6bn liability after jury deems Twitter tweets "materially false"
summary: Investing.com -- A California jury has delivered a stinging rebuke to Elon Musk, finding that the billionaire misled investors during the volatile lead-up to his $44 billion acquisition of the platform formerly known as Twitter.

title: Meet the 5 "Magnificent Seven" Stocks That Are Brilliant Buys Now
summary: Several Magnificent Seven stocks are down from their highs.

title: Uber fights to stay in the robotaxi race
summary: Will driverless taxis make Uber, which dominates human-led ridesharing, obsolete? There are certainly reasons for investors to wonder. After...

title: US jury finds Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders
summary: A federal jury in California found Friday that tech tycoon Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders, driving down the company's share price as he was poised to buy it in a $44 billion deal.The civil complaint in California accused Musk of driving down Twitter's stock price to gain leverage to renegotiate the purchase price or get out of the deal completely, causing people who sold shares to lose money.

title: Tesla Is Investing in xAI. Is That Good News for Investors?
summary: Tesla is investing billions in Elon Musk's AI start-up. This move could accelerate the company's ambitions in self-driving cars and robotics, but it also raises important questions for investors.
